question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicRepository.findById() returns a Topic with feeScheduleKey bytes that, when decoded as a protobuf Key, could be a KeyList with zero keys, meaning no signatures are required to authorize fee schedule updates, allowing the attacker to submit ConsensusUpdateTopic transactions that modify the fee schedule without any valid feeScheduleKey signature?
